narrative: OMX30 enters Monday in confirmed bull market structure: +7.3% above 200-MA, ADX 30.3 showing strong directional bias, MACD above signal line. Global backdrop is mixed (Europe flat to slightly negative, US marginally down Friday close), but Asia's overnight strength (Hang Seng +1.56%, Shanghai +1.16%) and commodity move (Brent +2.21%, copper +0.62%) provide positive tailwinds. Oil's jump is a double-edged sword—it lifts Industrials (cost headwind) and pressures Consumer spending, but copper strength directly benefits Boliden. Technically, RSI at 58 offers room to run; Stochastic at K=74.8/D=79.1 signals potential consolidation but not reversal. Key risk: US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET could trigger renewed risk-off if weaker than expected, and yields are already ebbing (10Y -0.46%), which may pressure bank NIM expansion narratives.
morningBrief: OMX30 likely opens +0.3 to +0.5% on positive Asia momentum and commodity tailwinds. Bull market setup intact: +7.3% above 200-MA, ADX 30.3, MACD bullish. Watch 3180 resistance (50-MA proxy); support at 3085 (Bollinger lower band). Industrials (Atlas Copco, Volvo B, Sandvik) face mixed signals—copper strength helps, but oil rise increases input cost anxiety; stay selective. Banks (SEB A, Handelsbanken A) may fade slightly if yields continue to compress (10Y already -0.46%)—NIM expansion thesis softens. Boliden is the standout beneficiary: copper +0.62% + gold's relative firmness. Exporters (Ericsson B, Hexagon B) remain under NASDAQ weakness pressure but could stabilize if risk sentiment holds. Watch for US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET—a miss could trigger late-session volatility. Maintain bullish bias but trim size into any +0.5%+ open; consolidation is healthy in this structure.
drivers: ADX 30.3 confirms strong bullish momentum; OMX30 +7.3% above 200-MA
Oil +2.21% to 73.58 supports industrial input dynamics; copper +0.62% benefits mining
Positive Asia close (Hang Seng +1.56%, Shanghai +1.16%) provides risk-on momentum heading into European open
EUR/SEK +0.19% to 11.09 supports OMX30 earnings translation; USD/SEK -0.16% neutral on exporter boost
RSI 58.3 neutral—no mean reversion warning; MACD above signal with +2.066 histogram confirms sustained bullish structure

sectorImpacts.reason: Oil +2.21% increases input costs, offsetting some upside from robust global demand signals; copper +0.62% provides minor tailwind to machinery and engineering subsegments.
US 10Y yield declined -0.46% to 4.372%; compression narrows NIM expansion expectations. Swedish bank NIM sensitivity is high to long-end repricing.
Copper +0.62% and gold stable; direct revenue uplift. Boliden is the OMX30's primary precious/base metals play.
NASDAQ -0.24% on Friday close creates headwind for sentiment-sensitive OMX tech names; however, ADX strength and bull market backdrop may contain downside.
Oil +2.21% pressures discretionary spending and increases EVO/consumer cost base; EUR/SEK +0.19% raises H&M sourcing costs slightly.
Yield decline (-0.46%) is mildly negative for refinancing costs, but modest magnitude limits impact in current risk-on environment.
Risk-on global backdrop limits defensive rotation boost; however, these stocks remain stable anchors in any late-session volatility (US Jobless Claims risk).

econCalendarAlert: No major Swedish economic releases today. US Jobless Claims (Initial + Continued) at 14:30 CET—potential volatility catalyst if data misses consensus. Monitor for ECB speaker commentary.
topRisk: US Jobless Claims miss (weakness) could trigger risk-off flight, with OMX30 losing +0.5% in afternoon session. Secondary risk: further yield compression (10Y already -0.46%) erodes bank sector support. Oil volatility above 74 could stress industrials' margin narratives.
technicalSummary: ADX 30.3 (strong directional bias); MACD above signal, histogram +2.066
Bollinger %B=0.66 (upper half but not extended); width 4.89% indicates moderate volatility
Stochastic K=74.8/D=79.1: potential consolidation signal, not reversal; neutral
52-week: 91% of range, -2.0% from highs—limited downside buffer
ROC-90 +1.1% neutral—momentum is steady but not accelerating
